schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: A Cairn-native device that is CC's self-serve path to root. A command goes in; it runs as root; a permanent JSON audit record comes out (the command line, the return code, stdout, stderr — the four Akien named — plus an id + date that make it a locatable, expiring record of truth). The audit folder self-cycles to a rolling one-month window (`retention_days`, default 31). Akien's whole ops role shrinks to TWO acts: (1) START the daemon (`python3 cairn/sudo_relay/daemon.py`) — one `sudo -v` password prompt, which IS his consent — and (2) approve. `SudoRelayDevice` subclasses `BaseDevice` (carries CP1-CP6, reports the Form v0 #2 surface). Its capability is `request_root(command)`; the daemon (`daemon.py`) is the one privileged process, holding sudo warm and running each pending command via `root_executor` (`sudo -n bash -c`).
why: The founding measurement: get Akien out of running the system. Today CC reaches root through the UU-tree daemon — a STOPGAP Cairn must not depend on at runtime; this device is its Cairn-native replacement, built fresh to Form v0 like the tester and db_domain. Scope RATIFIED by Akien (2026-07-18): UNRESTRICTED root — 'the box IS the restriction' (a dedicated single-purpose box, careful environmental constraint, six months of calibration), NOT an allowlist and NOT policy-hesitation in CC; the safeguard is the VISIBLE audit + transparency, not friction (CC memory [[constraint-is-the-box-not-hesitation]]).
invoke: Akien starts it: `python3 cairn/sudo_relay/daemon.py` (Ctrl-C releases root). Watch the window: `python3 cairn/sudo_relay/daemon.py --status`. CC uses it: `from cairn.sudo_relay.relay import request_root; request_root('<shell command>')` → returns the audit record. Prove: `python3 cairn/sudo_relay/proofs/test_sudo_relay.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: device — a runtime component on the spine; a 'thing-for-akien-along-the-way' (it unblocks CC self-serving root, which is why the spine paused here).
entry: FRESH design to Form v0; the relay MECHANISM is sound quarry from the UU sudoer_daemon (`UnseenUniversity/.../igor/tools/sudoer_daemon.sh`): the consent model (Akien runs the daemon once = one password; keepalive holds it; `sudo -k` revokes on exit) and the file protocol (pending -> executing -> done, atomic handoff) cross as pattern. What is NEW and Cairn's: the record of truth (six fields, stdout/stderr kept FULL per Law 7), the rolling-month retention, the injected-executor SEAM that makes the whole thing provable without root, and TEMPORARY-AS-PHYSICS with a VISIBLE window (the UU daemon's fatal flaw — it held live sudo 4+ days because its lifetime was invisible — made physics here: a hard 24h cap = 'log in once a day' as the sole automatic release, a heartbeat status surface, and a keepalive that keeps announcing the remaining window; Ctrl-C is the manual stop-sooner. NO idle timeout by default — one login lasts the day; the dedicated box is the safety net, not a timeout, per the ratified scope that rejects friction; idle is an opt-in knob).
state: PROVED for the minimal stone (all green without root): the protocol runs a command and returns the true exit code + stdout + stderr (in-process AND across two real processes, live-measured); the record is permanent and complete (exactly six fields, stdout kept FULL); the folder cycles after a month and not before, never touching a foreign file; temporary is physics (past the absolute cap OR idle past the timeout -> expire; fresh + active -> hold); the window is visible and honest (a live pid reads live, a dead pid reads stale, never laundered into 'up'). Dogfooded UNDER the tester (green VALIDATION). A falsification tooth confirmed to bite (laundering the exit code to 0 reds the protocol proof). The daemon's `--status` path is honest with no daemon running. OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) the daemon's sudo acquisition + keepalive (`daemon.py`) is the ONE privileged part the proof cannot exercise (it needs a password/TTY) — everything UNDER it is proven; the daemon is a thin wrapper wiring those to `root_executor`; the live root run is Akien's one act (his consent, by design); (b) approval is today the COARSE act of choosing to run the daemon (consent = the password act, as in the UU relay) — per-command approval / an allowlist is deliberately NOT built (ratified scope is unrestricted root); (c) one command at a time (atomic pending->executing), no queue/concurrency — matches a single builder's serial use; (d) not yet wired into instance-space provisioning beyond lazy dir-creation on first use.
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_sudo_relay.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) `process_pending` runs a command but the returned/audited return code, stdout, or stderr does not match what the command actually produced (the relay must not lie about what happened under root); (2) an audit record's field-set is not exactly the ratified six, or stdout/stderr is truncated (a record of truth must be permanent and complete, Law 7); (3) the retention sweep deletes a within-the-month record, keeps an over-a-month one, or touches a file it did not mint; (4) `should_expire` fails to expire past the absolute cap (24h — the sole automatic release by default), or expires a fresh daemon within the cap, or fires the opt-in idle timeout when none is set (temporary must be physics — root cannot be held past a day, nor dropped mid-day on a walk-away when the intent is 'log in once a day'); (5) `daemon_status` reports a dead/absent daemon as live (the root-holding window must be visible and honest, never assumed); (6) `SudoRelayDevice` stops subclassing `BaseDevice` / drops CP1-CP6, or `introspect()` stops reporting intention -> state -> settings in order.
how_it_learns: Yes — two ways. (1) The AUDIT is the learning substrate: every root run leaves a permanent record (command, code, stdout, stderr), so the trail of what root was used for is greppable evidence, not memory — the raw material for calibrating the box over the ratified six-month window (which commands recur, which fail, what CC actually needs root FOR). Akien's scope decision is explicitly a learning stance: start unrestricted + a dedicated box, and let the audit teach what constraint (if any) the evidence warrants — the opposite of guessing an allowlist up front. (2) The daemon's WINDOW is a standing measurement: the visible hard-cap deadline (and the keepalive that keeps announcing it) exists precisely because the UU daemon TAUGHT US (by holding root 4+ days) that an invisible lifetime is the defect; this device folds that lesson in as physics. A same-session correction is itself a specimen of this: the first cut carried a 30-min idle timeout (CC's own over-add), which Akien caught as misaligned with 'log in once a day' — the idle default was removed (opt-in now), leaving the 24h cap as the sole automatic release. Its own horizon: the cap (24h) and the retention window are best-guesses-with-an-expiry — settings, tuned as the audit shows the real cadence of use.
traces_to: founding intention (get Akien out of running the system — this unblocks CC self-serving root)
Law 4 (temporary + the owner-gate are physics, not policy — the daemon enforces the window)
Law 6 (CC owns the code; the daemon's authority is Akien's, held only while he runs it)
Law 7 (the audit is a record of truth — errors permanent, stdout/stderr uncollapsed)
Law 3 (the visible window is measured — live-pid check, not the mere presence of a file)
Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share this address)
Law 8 (proven by a test a hollow relay could not pass — the executor seam proves it without root)
CC memory [[constraint-is-the-box-not-hesitation]] (unrestricted by design; box + audit are the safeguard)
CC memory [[sudo-relay-self-serve-ops]] (the UU stopgap this replaces)
Form v0 #2 (the ordered introspection surface, MAP.md:157)
owner: CC owns the sudo_relay code + its proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the AUTHORITY the daemon carries — it exists only while he chooses to run it, and the password act is his alone; the daemon holds no stored credential. The audit log is a record of truth written by the relay process (as Akien, not as root).
proof: proofs/test_sudo_relay.py — the protocol runs the command and returns the truth (exit code + stdout + stderr propagate); the record is permanent and complete (exactly six fields, stdout kept FULL, round-trips on disk); the folder cycles after a month but not before, and never touches a foreign file; temporary is physics (cap + idle -> expire, fresh -> hold); the window is visible and honest (live vs stale pid); device-hood + the six + Form v0 #2 order. All green without root (injected `local_executor` seam), and green under the tester. Live cross-process check (non-root executor) confirms the on-disk client<->daemon handoff. Green: `python3 cairn/sudo_relay/proofs/test_sudo_relay.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-18 as a 'thing-for-akien-along-the-way' — the reason the code spine paused after db_domain: the spine's remaining steps want CC self-serving root, and the UU-tree relay CC used for that is a stopgap Cairn must not depend on at runtime. Replaces that stopgap with a Cairn-native device. Next after this: the spine resumes (MAP.md:888) — ground loop -> rack -> inference domain -> web server. Deferred until pulled: per-command approval / allowlist (ratified scope is unrestricted), a pending queue, tighter instance-space provisioning.
